releases.notes: fix(ci): use GITHUB_TOKEN for gh release view, PUBLISH_TOKEN only for dispatch

Wave 1 — correctness:
- Hilt: drop manual @Provides, use @param:ApplicationContext on local stores
- AutoRefreshController: cancel interval-off collector inside refreshJob
- TripRepository: bound stop_times cache (LRU 32, TTL 6 h, mutex-guarded)
- AppUpdateManager: 30-min cool-off so cold start doesn't always hit network
- ServerClock: reject implausible drift (>2 h), log failures
- MainActivity: replace runBlocking on DataStore with lifecycleScope
- StopDataRepository: drop empty migration noop on every version bump
Wave 2 — architecture:
- Centralize Json via SerializationModule
- Inject API tokens via @GolemioApiKey / @GitHubReleaseToken / @GitHubIssuesToken
  qualifiers; data layer no longer reads BuildConfig
- GolemioApiService: drop manual decode, rely on Ktor ContentNegotiation
- NetworkModule: HttpTimeout, HttpRequestRetry, drop prettyPrint
- ApkInstaller: timeouts, bodyAsChannel().copyTo, FileProvider scoped to updates/
- StopSearchRepository: gain findByExactName / findNearby / ensureCache so
  NearbyViewModel no longer reaches into StopCache directly
- Move AutoRefreshController and TripDetailController to ui/util
Wave 3 — build infra:
- Java 17 source/target + jvmTarget (was 1.8)
- Release builds: minify + shrinkResources on, proguard-rules.pro added
- Fail loudly on missing keystore env vars in assembleRelease/bundleRelease
- Manifest: data extraction + backup rules excluding datastore/
- CI: split PR (debug + lint) from master push (lint + release + bundle)
Wave 4 — UI/Compose:
- staticCompositionLocalOf with required-error defaults
- Memoize partition+sort in PlatformDepartureRows; parse ISO once per departure
- Fix Icons.Filled.Accessible deprecation, drop dead !! derefs
- Externalize hardcoded English strings; TripDetailUiState.Error and
  ReportState.Error now carry @StringRes IDs
Wave 5 — polish:
- Replace Math.pow version comparator with compareVersions-based one
- Drop stale TODO and unused StopCache.isEmpty
Removed the Upload APK artifact step. The same APK and AAB are already
attached to the GitHub Release as assets, and the release-publisher
dispatch reads from the release, so the Actions artifact was redundant
and was hitting GitHub's storage quota — blocking v1.9.5 from cutting
a release.
Added a Prune old workflow artifacts step at the start of the build
that keeps the newest 3 artifacts and deletes the rest. Defensive
against any future workflow that uploads artifacts.
Bumps to v1.9.6 since v1.9.5 never published; the artifact-quota
failure prevented Create Release from running.
fix(nearby): per-mode distance + per-mode estimator [release][publish]
Two related improvements to the Nearby screen and trip detail sheet.
Nearby distance per mode group: when grouping is off, each mode row
(e.g. "Olovnice - Bus" vs "Olovnice - Train") now shows the distance
to the closest platform serving that mode, computed from the API
platformLocations response. Falls back to the cache distance when GPS
or platform locations are unavailable. Also refines distance for
grouped stops since a name can span platforms hundreds of meters apart.
Vehicle position estimator parameterized by transport type: dwell and
transition durations now scale with mode (longer dwells for trains,
faster transitions for metro). Threaded TransportType through
TripDetail, TripDetailController, and the minimized state so restore
preserves the value. Replay grid script updated to match.
fix(ui): align route numbers in departure rows
Fixed min width on route number text so single, double, and triple
digit route names align headsign text consistently.
fix(nearby): sort favorite mode groups first in split stops (#86)
API calls deduplicated by stop name. Mode groups sorted so favorited
key appears before non-favorited siblings. All checks use exact key
comparison — no base-name fallbacks.

Includes v1.9.1 (refactoring) and v1.9.2 (bugfixes #82, #83, #84).
fix: dialog labels, first stop line, pull-to-refresh feedback (#82, #83, #84)
#82: Group stops dialog used "Grant/Deny" from permission strings.
     Now uses dedicated "Switch/Cancel" strings.
#83: Route view drew a line above the first stop. Added isFirst guard.
#84: Pull-to-refresh showed no spinner on NoLocation/NothingInRange
     states. Now passes isRefreshing from ViewModel.
refactor: deduplicate logic, fix imports, improve encapsulation
Extract TripDetailController, GroupDeparturesByModeUseCase, and
VersionUtils to eliminate copy-pasted code across ViewModels.
Replace all FQN references with proper imports, remove wildcard
imports, eliminate unsafe !! assertions, privatize ViewModel
internals behind saveAnnotation(), fix misnamed Settings route,
share Json instance in GolemioApiService.

feat(stops): refactor stopName to stopKey with mode-aware departures
Rename StopData.stopName to stopKey throughout all layers. Stop keys
encode transport mode (e.g. "Ohrada|TRAM") enabling per-mode favorites,
hidden stops, and filtered departure boards.
- Departure board splits into mode cards when grouping is off
- Search results show mode-split cards based on setting
- Favorites stored separately per grouping mode (swap on toggle)
- Fix navigation tab stuck state when pendingSearchRoute persists
[release][publish]
feat(nearby): split stops by transport mode setting (#60)
"Group stops by name" toggle in Settings (default: on). When off,
stops with multiple transport types get separate cards with suffix
(e.g., "Olovnice — Bus", "Olovnice — Train"). Favorites/hidden
check both display name and base name for compatibility.
feat(annotation): include user name from report settings
feat(update): show "What's new" dialog on version update [release][publish]
Messages tied to specific versions in UpdateMessages.kt. On first
launch after update, shows dialog with all messages from versions
between last seen and current. Gate GitHub Release on [release] tag,
separate from [publish] for WhatsApp notifications.
feat(tools): add /annotation skill and evaluation framework
- /annotation skill: fetch new annotations, save locally, close issues, evaluate
- evaluate.py: replay all saved annotations against current and old model params
- Save annotation issues #74, #75, #76 as local JSON files

feat(trip): tune position model from annotation data [publish]
Parameters tuned against 67 annotations from 2 real tram rides:
- TRANSITION_MS: 15s → 20s (longer departing/arriving phases)
- MAX_ALPHA: 1.0 → 0.85 (never fully snap to API)
- MIN_API_AGE_MS: 0 → 10s (smooth API corrections)
- Add app version to annotation data
- Save tuning results and replay script in model-tuning/
Backward jumps reduced 60-70%, at-stop accuracy +5%, no backward guard
(tested — hurts accuracy more than it helps).

fix(trip): correct phase layout and stale state in position model [publish]
- Reorder phases: AT_STOP is now last in segment (vehicle dwells
  before departure), DEPARTING is first (vehicle just left)
- Fix stale captured values in tick loop with rememberUpdatedState
- No green blinking when vehicle is not tracked (schedule-only mode)
- Annotation upload on bar dismiss (trigger: manual) + 30min auto-stale
- Annotation issue body includes trigger type

fix(scanner): drop YouTube IFrame entirely + close diagonal seam in plane tiles [release] [publish]
Two more fixes from screenshot:
- Removed <script src=youtube/iframe_api>. Every card has a local mp3,
  so the YT player was unused dead weight that kept leaking a giant
  play overlay onto the scan view (despite earlier attempts to hide
  it). Player init guarded by ytReady which now stays false forever.
  If a YT-only card ever appears in cards.json we'll bring the script
  back; until then, no overlay.
- The cover image on each tile was rendered as two affine-mapped
  triangles. The shared diagonal showed a 1px black seam from
  sub-pixel rounding. Outset each triangle's clip by 1px along the
  outward normal so the two triangles overlap a hair across the
  diagonal — hides the seam.
fix(scanner): hide stray YouTube play overlay; force camera fullscreen [release] [publish]
Two visual bugs reported on v0.1.8 with screenshot:
1. The YouTube IFrame host was 200×200 at top:-220px, supposed to be
   off-screen, but Android Chrome was bringing the giant play
   overlay back into view. Reduce to a 1×1 px host with opacity
   0.01 + iframe sized to 1px so the overlay can't render at any
   visible size while still passing autoplay heuristics.
2. MindAR's <video> rendered in a small box top-left instead of
   filling the screen, because the WebView's clientWidth/Height
   reading mid-launch was off. Add !important position/size CSS so
   the camera always covers the viewport regardless of MindAR's
   computed offsets.

fix(scanner): hide stray YouTube play overlay; force camera fullscreen [release] [publish]
Two visual bugs reported on v0.1.8 with screenshot:
1. The YouTube IFrame host was 200×200 at top:-220px, supposed to be
   off-screen, but Android Chrome was bringing the giant play
   overlay back into view. Reduce to a 1×1 px host with opacity
   0.01 + iframe sized to 1px so the overlay can't render at any
   visible size while still passing autoplay heuristics.
2. MindAR's <video> rendered in a small box top-left instead of
   filling the screen, because the WebView's clientWidth/Height
   reading mid-launch was off. Add !important position/size CSS so
   the camera always covers the viewport regardless of MindAR's
   computed offsets.
feat(scanner): fade camera feed in from black over 1s [release] [publish]
The grey-and-blank first video frame popped jarringly when entering
scan mode. Add a CSS opacity transition on body > video so it ramps
0 → 1 over 1 second after MindAR starts. UI elements (home icon,
hint, reticle) appear immediately at full opacity above the fading
camera. Reset on exitScanBtn so re-entering re-fades.

perf: ship 512² fronts + 256² cover, drop canvas DPR cap [release] [publish]
Two changes that compound:
- scanner/build.py now downsizes data/ → scanner/art/ via Pillow
  Lanczos. Originals at 1024² stay in data/ for print PDFs (printed
  card quality unaffected). scanner/art/ shrinks 46MB → 9.6MB.
  Card fronts: 512², ~530KB each. Cover: 256², ~107KB.
- scanner/animations/plane.js: DPR cap was already 1, kept that way.
  Each textured-tile draw now samples a 256² source instead of 1024²,
  a 16× cheaper texture sample, and the canvas backing buffer is
  4× smaller than at native DPR.
Net: plane animation should run smoothly during the 5s glide on
mid-range phones. APK shrinks correspondingly.
